http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#ESPDR.label: ESPD Request
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#ESPDR.description: A document issued by a Buyer defining how the Exclusion Grounds and the Selection Criteria should be addressed in an European Single Procurement Document for a specific procurement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.label: specifies Procurement Criterion
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.description: Relation indicating the Procurement Criterion to be used.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Notic.label: Notice
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Notic.description: Document published by the Buyer about market opportunities and results.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Proce.label: Procedure
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Proce.description: A legally defined set of administrative activities conducted to conclude one or more Contracts. Additional Information The Procedure is categorised in the law according to different rules determining whether the Procedure is Open, Restricted, Negotiated with or without publicity, etc. (see Procedure Type).

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#descr.label: describes Objective Participation Rules
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#descr.description: General explanatory text on the rules and criteria to be fulfilled by the Tender. (epo:ParticipationConditionsSummary)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#descr.label: describes Verification Method
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#descr.description: General explanatory text on the means used to validate the Participation Conditions. (epo:ParticipationConditionsSummary)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasRe.label: has Reserved Procurement
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasRe.description: Explanation as to whether a procurement may be reserved for the participation of certain types of organisation.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#NonDi.label: Non Disclosure Agreement Term
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#NonDi.description: Conditions and stipulations about a non-disclosure agreement. Additional information: A non-disclosure agreement is a Contract between different parties which ensures information is not shared with other parties.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isNon.label: is Non Disclosure Agreement Required
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isNon.description: A non-disclosure agreement is needed. Additional information: A non-disclosure agreement is a Contract between different parties which ensures information is not shared with other parties. (epo:NonDisclosureAgreementTerm)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Auxil.label: Auxiliary Party
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Auxil.description: Role of an Agent who may be mentioned in the information exchanged during the Procurement Process but who does not play an active part in it.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Docum.label: Documentation
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Docum.description: A structured collection of records, reports, or supporting materials that provide context or explanation regarding a process, requirement, or decision within a procurement activity.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Selec.label: Selected Candidate List
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Selec.description: Record of Candidates admitted to take part in award phases of procurements.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Candi.label: Candidate
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Candi.description: The Role of an Agent that has sought an invitation or has been invited to take part in a restricted Procedure, in a competitive Procedure with negotiation, in a negotiated Procedure without prior publication, in a competitive dialogue or in an innovation partnership.

http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Interval.label: Time interval
http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Interval.description: A temporal entity with an extent or duration

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Envir.label: Environmental Emission Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Envir.description: Information about the emissions related to a product, work or service that are released into the environment.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCa.label: has Calculation Method
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCa.description: Formula for obtaining values. Additional Information: The possible values are monetary values, ranking scores, criterion weighting. (epo-cat:EnvironmentalEmissionInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEn.label: has Environmental Emission Code
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEn.description: Relation indicating the Environmental Emission Code.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasMe.label: has Measure
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasMe.description: Relation indicating the unit used for stating the size.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Abstr.label: Abstract Item
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Abstr.description: Gathering class for the Items, Item Requests and Deliverables. Additional information: The subclasses have in common that they are related to a procurement need.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quant.label: Quantity
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quant.description: A counted number of non-monetary units possibly including fractions.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Trans.label: Transport Means
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Trans.description: Description of a particular vehicle or vessel used for the conveyance of goods or persons.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Fund.label: Fund
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Fund.description: A financial resource used to support the Procurement. Additional Information: In the context of EU, Funds can be divided into programmes, actions and projects. Examples of EU funds are: the European Structural and Investment Funds, European Social Fund (ESF), the Connecting Europe Facility (CEF) programme, or the ISA2 programme and its actions (e.g. Action 2016.05 European Public Procurement Initiative, which supports the eProcurement Ontology under sub-action 3). Funds may change between the lot and the contract, for example in the case of an emergency crisis, a contract may be financed by a budget that was not foreseen in the call.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasFu.label: has Fund Programme
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasFu.description: Relation indicating the EU programme used for funding.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasUR.label: has URL
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasUR.description: The identifier of a resource. Additional Information For example: 1. The URL of the system from where to access the procurement documents; 2. The URL of the system for the submission of tender documents; 3. The URL of the system from where to download a tool to communicate with the Buyer; 4. The URL of the system used by a Technique to allow Economic Operators to exchange information with the Buyer (e.g. eAuction and DPS Systems) 5. The URL of the system used to exchange information between Buyer and EO for questions and clarifications; (cccev:Evidence) The identifier of a resource. Additional Information For example: 1. The URL of the system from where to access the procurement documents; 2. The URL of the system for the submission of tender documents; 3. The URL of the system from where to download a tool to communicate with the Buyer; 4. The URL of the system used by a Technique to allow Economic Operators to exchange information with the Buyer (e.g. eAuction and DPS Systems) 5. The URL of the system used to exchange information between Buyer and EO for questions and clarifications; (epo:Fund)

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Procu.label: Procurement Process Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Procu.description: Information about the temporal unfolding or succession of Procurement Objects.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isCom.label: is Competition Terminated
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isCom.description: No further contracts will be awarded in this procedure. Additional Information: This can be instantiated in the post award phase. PIN for Competition needs to be signaled. This field can be used even if no contracts are awarded in the contract award notice. (epo:ProcurementProcessInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isDPS.label: is DPS Terminated
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isDPS.description: End of the Dynamic Purchase System (DPS). Additional Information: This property can be used in the contract award notice even if no contracts are awarded. (epo:ProcurementProcessInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isToB.label: is To Be Relaunched
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isToB.description: Indicator of whether the procurement object is to be relaunched. Additional information: This can be instantiated in the post award phase. (epo:ProcurementProcessInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Lot.label: Lot
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Lot.description: A qualitative, quantitative or strategic subdivision of the goods, services or works to be procured, allowing the award of one or more Contracts.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Proce.label: Procedure
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Proce.description: A legally defined set of administrative activities conducted to conclude one or more Contracts. Additional Information The Procedure is categorised in the law according to different rules determining whether the Procedure is Open, Restricted, Negotiated with or without publicity, etc. (see Procedure Type).

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Exclu.label: Exclusion Ground
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Exclu.description: Criterion that describes a legal Requirement to be met by the Economic Operator to be a Candidate in the Procurement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEx.label: has Exclusion Ground Type
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEx.description: Relation indicating the exclusion ground classification.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Secur.label: Security Clearance Term
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Secur.description: Conditions and stipulations about the status requested of individuals allowing them access to classified information (state or organisational secrets) or to restricted areas, after completion of a thorough background check.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDe.label: has Deadline
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDe.description: The time limit by which the security clearance must be submitted to the buyer. (epo:SecurityClearanceTerm)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#cover.label: covers All Selection Criteria
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#cover.description: Indicator that the Certificate proves whether the Organization fulfils all Selection Criteria. (epo-sub:EvidenceInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#summa.label: summarises Information About Evidence
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#summa.description: Relation indicating the Certificate that the information refers to.

http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Evidence.label: Evidence
http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Evidence.description: The Evidence class is defined in the Core Criterion and Core Evidence vocabulary (CCCEV) as any resource that can prove that a specific requirement is met.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Contr.label: Contractor
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Contr.description: The Role of an Agent that has signed a Contract with a Buyer.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#fores.label: foresees Subcontractor
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#fores.description: Relation indicating that an Organization plans to have a Subcontractor. Additional information: In eForms, in a Result Notice, the foreseen Subcontractors in a Tender are associated to the Organization that proposes the Subcontractors. The Subcontractor is associated to the Organization, rather than the Tenderer, so that, in the case of multiple Organizations being a Tenderer, it is clear to which Organization the proposed Subcontractor is associated.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#needs.label: needs To Be A Winner
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#needs.description: The Contractor must be a Winner.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Subco.label: Subcontractor
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Subco.description: A Role of an Agent that has an agreement to perform part or all of the obligations of another Agents's Contract. Additional information For some Procedures, the Subcontractor signs as well the Contract between the Buyer and the Contractor. At tendering time, entities relied upon by the Economic Operators can be Subcontractors or not. When modelling ESPD we will analyze whether we need or not a Role named "relied upon".

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Winne.label: Winner
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Winne.description: A Role of an Agent to whom a Lot is awarded.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Dynam.label: Dynamic Purchase System Technique
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Dynam.description: A Technique that allows the selection of Candidates throughout the Procedure via the Qualification Criteria, followed by individual Mini-Competitions for the Award of Purchase Contracts.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDP.label: has DPS Scope
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDP.description: Explanation as to whether a dps is used and by whom. The codelist to be used is at-voc:dps-usage which is available at http://publications.europa.eu/resource/dataset/dps-usage

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEs.label: has Estimated Duration
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEs.description: Relation indicating a Dynamic Purchase System has an estimated Duration.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasUn.label: has Unknown Duration
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasUn.description: Indicates the Duration is undetermined. (epo:ContractTerm) Indicates the Duration is undetermined. (epo:DynamicPurchaseSystemTechnique)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasUn.label: has Unlimited Duration
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasUn.description: Indicates the Duration is endless. (epo:ContractTerm) Indicates the Duration is endless. (epo:DynamicPurchaseSystemTechnique)

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Duration.label: Time duration
http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Duration.description: Duration of a temporal extent expressed as a number scaled by a temporal unit

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Shipm.label: Shipment Agreement
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Shipm.description: The commercial agreement between the Buyer and the Seller. Additional Information: The Shipment Agreement describes the items being shipped.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Buyer.label: Buyer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Buyer.description: A Role of an Agent that awards a Contract and/or purchases items. Additional information: In Public Procurement the Role of Buyer is carried out by the following types of Organisation: Contracting Authority, Contracting Entity, a defense Contractor, an international Organisation, or an organisation awarding a Contract subsidized by a Contracting Authority. In Pre-Award, the Buyer generally awards the Contract, however future purchasers may be foreseen. In Post-Award the buyer generally refers to the purchaser of items.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Selle.label: Seller
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Selle.description: A Role of an Agent who transfers the ownership of the Procurement results (goods, services or works) to the Buyer. Additional information: A Role of an Agent that sells a Procurement Result (goods, services or work) to a Buyer. The Seller is bound by a Contract i.e. it has legal responsibility. The Seller may or may not be the same as the Supplier. The Seller may or may not issue the Invoice. The Seller may or may not be the owner of the credit owed by the Buyer.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Techn.label: Technique
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Techn.description: Method used for conducting Procurement Procedures. Several Techniques can be combined in one single Procurement Procedure (e.g. eAuction can be carried out in a Framework Agreement or DPS).

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEs.label: has Estimated Validity Period
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEs.description: Relation indicating the time range in which the instance of the concept is valid.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasVa.label: has Validity Period
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasVa.description: The relation indicating until when a given instance of a concept is applicable.

http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Interval.label: Time interval
http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Interval.description: A temporal entity with an extent or duration

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Payme.label: Payment Card Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Payme.description: Information about card used for payment contemporaneous with invoice issuance.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.label: has Payment Card Holder Name
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.description: The name of the account holder of the card used for payment. Additional Information: An account holder is the entity listed or identified as the holder of a Financial Account by the Financial Institution. (epo-inv:PaymentCardInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.label: has Payment Card Primary Account Number
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.description: The series of digits embossed on the front of a card used for payment. (epo-inv:PaymentCardInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Asset.label: Asset
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Asset.description: Resource necessary for the provision of a service.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPr.label: has Predominance
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPr.description: Estimated percentage-share of Assets provided compared to Assets used for activities other than the public services. (epo:Asset)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSi.label: has Significance
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSi.description: Estimated percentage-share of Assets provided in overall Assets used to provide services under Contract. (epo:Asset)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Buyer.label: Buyer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Buyer.description: A Role of an Agent that awards a Contract and/or purchases items. Additional information: In Public Procurement the Role of Buyer is carried out by the following types of Organisation: Contracting Authority, Contracting Entity, a defense Contractor, an international Organisation, or an organisation awarding a Contract subsidized by a Contracting Authority. In Pre-Award, the Buyer generally awards the Contract, however future purchasers may be foreseen. In Post-Award the buyer generally refers to the purchaser of items.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#deleg.label: delegates Ancillary Activities To
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#deleg.description: Entrusts ancillary purchasing activities to ProcurementServiceProvider.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAc.label: has Activity Description
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAc.description: In the ePO ontology a taxonomy with all activities, based on different classifications (COFOG, UTILITIES, NACE), will be provided. In ePO this field is to be used exclusively to complement the definition attached to the MainActivityCode. However, in eForms there is the code "other" to cover undefined activities. For mapping to this eForms feature one could also use this property. (epo:Buyer)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBu.label: has Buyer Profile
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBu.description: Website address where the buyer publishes information on its procurement procedures and general information. (epo:Buyer)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isCon.label: is Contracting Entity
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isCon.description: Role of entities, which: (a) are contracting authorities or public undertakings and which pursue one of the activities referred to in Articles 8 to 14 of the Directive 2014/25/EU. (b) when they are not contracting authorities or public undertakings, have as one of their activities any of the activities referred to in Articles 8 to 14, or any combination thereof and operate on the basis of special or exclusive rights granted by a competent authority of a Member State. Additional Information: The indicator is needed in order to discriminate between those contracts where the Contracting Entity acts as a Contracting Authority undergoing the limits and the rules of Directive 24 and those where it acts as a Contracting Entity, with certain relaxed constraints. For example, the Contracting Entities have different thresholds for the application of Directive 24 if compared with Contracting Authorities. (epo:Buyer)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Procu.label: Procurement Service Provider
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Procu.description: Role of a public or private body which offers ancillary purchasing activities on the market. Additional information: "Ancillary Purchasing Activities" means activities consisting in the provision of support to purchasing activities, in particular in the following forms: (a) technical infrastructure enabling Contracting Authorities to award Public Contracts or to conclude Framework Agreements for works, supplies or services; (b) advice on the conduct or design of public Procurement Procedures; (c) preparation and management of Procurement Procedures on behalf and for the account of the Contracting Authority concerned; This would be equivalent to the concept of 'ProcuringEntity' in OCDS: 'The Entity managing the Procurement, which may be different from the Buyer who is paying / using the items being procured'.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSpe.label: is Specific To Order Response Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSpe.description: Information that is particular to an Order Response Line.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Response Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: Details concerning the Seller's ability to fulfil a given Order Line. Additional Information: The Order Response Line is used to inform on the acceptance or rejection of an Order Line or to make a counter-offer.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Direc.label: Direct Contract
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Direc.description: A Contract resulting from Lot Award Outcome. Additional information: A Framework Agreement is not a Direct Contract.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#resul.label: results From Lot Award Outcome
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#resul.description: Relation to indicate that a given type of Contract is a consequence of a given Lot Award Outcome.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotAw.label: Lot Award Outcome
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotAw.description: Result concerning the Lot that was attributed by the Awarder.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Frame.label: Framework Agreement
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Frame.description: An agreement between one or more Contracting Authorities and one or more Economic Operators. Additional Information: When a Framework Agreement is concerned, the FA will result from LotAwardOutcome which describes a Lot which specifies the both the Qualification and Award Criteria.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#resul.label: results From Lot Award Outcome
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#resul.description: Relation to indicate that a given type of Contract is a consequence of a given Lot Award Outcome.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotAw.label: Lot Award Outcome
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotAw.description: Result concerning the Lot that was attributed by the Awarder.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Line.label: Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Line.description: Component of a document providing specific information.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAd.label: has Additional Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAd.description: Supplementary data about the instance of the concept. (epo-cat:Line)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Abstr.label: Abstract Item
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Abstr.description: Gathering class for the Items, Item Requests and Deliverables. Additional information: The subclasses have in common that they are related to a procurement need.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Docum.label: Document
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Docum.description: A set of interrelated Business Information representing the Business facts and associated metadata. The information may be conveyed in any language, medium or form, including textual, numerical, graphic, cartographic, audio-visual forms, etc.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.label: Certification Label
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.description: Concept that stands for a set of norms, expectations, standards and policies. Additional Information: Such standards may relate to social, ethical and quality etc.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasLa.label: has Label Type
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasLa.description: The label type such environmental, quality, social etc.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasRe.label: has Reference URI
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasRe.description: A reference to where the label specification (norms, expectations, standards and policies) can be found. (epo:CertificationLabel)

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Conce.label: Concession Contract
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Conce.description: A Contract between one or more Buyers and one or more Economic Operators giving the right to the Economic Operators to exploit the rights foreseen in the Contract which may include the receipt of Payments.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#resul.label: results From Lot Award Outcome
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#resul.description: Relation to indicate that a given type of Contract is a consequence of a given Lot Award Outcome.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotAw.label: Lot Award Outcome
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotAw.description: Result concerning the Lot that was attributed by the Awarder.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Revie.label: Review Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Revie.description: Information about the Review Decision or the Review Request.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEl.label: has Element Reference
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEl.description: Reference to the class instance in the current notice. (epo:ReviewInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#relat.label: relates To E Form Section Identifier
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#relat.description: Is about a specific eForms section.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Revie.label: Review Request
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Revie.description: Information about requests to review procedures.

http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.label: Identifier
http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.description: This class is based on the UN/CEFACT Identifier complex type defined in See Section 5.8 of Core Components Data Type Catalogue Version 3.1 (http://www.unece.org/fileadmin/DAM/cefact/codesfortrade/CCTS/CCTS-DTCatalogueVersion3p1.pdf) In RDF this is expressed using the following properties: - the content string should be provided using skos:notation, datatyped with the identifier scheme (inclduing the version number if appropriate); - use dcterms:creator to link to a class describing the agency that manages the identifier scheme or adms:schemaAgency to provide the name as a literal. Although not part of the ADMS conceptual model, it may be useful to provide further properties to the Identifier class such as dcterms:created to provide the date on which the identifier was issued.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#PreMa.label: Pre Market Consultation Notice
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#PreMa.description: Announcement of a pre-market consultation. Additional information: A pre-market consultation is not a call for competition and no procurement documents are available at the time of consultation. A consultation may or may not be followed by a future competition.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#prepa.label: prepares For Potential Procurement Object
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#prepa.description: Gathers information for a possible Procurement Object.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Agent.label: Agent In Role
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Agent.description: Relative concept that ties an Agent to a part they play in a given Situational Context. Additional information: The classification of roles is based is based on the degree of involvement into the Procurement Process: - Primary (Procurement) Pole is directly involved in the Procurement Process; - Secondary (Procurement sub-) Role is secondary to the Procurement Process - Tertiary (Related) Role is not involved in the Procurement Process.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Procu.label: Procurement Object
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Procu.description: The whole or a division of goods, services or works to be procured. Additional Information: Anything that can specify the procurement content (i.e. goods, services, work) is a Procurement Object. In a sense, such an "object" can constitute the "object of a Contract". To test whether something is a Procedure Object check if it can have a Purpose and/or CPV classification (The CPV establishes a single classification system for public procurement aimed at standardising the references used by Contracting Authorities and entities to describe the subject of Procurement Contracts.). Note: Procedure, seems to be an exception from this rule. Because it is a conflated term: it carries process properties and "purpose" properties.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Deliv.label: Delivery Agreement
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Deliv.description: Term applying to the delivery of goods, services and works. Additional Information: Delivery terms identifier can normally be Incoterms accompanied by the description of specific conditions related to the delivery.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasGe.label: has General Delivery Term
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasGe.description: Terms and conditions related to the delivery of goods. (epo-ord:DeliveryAgreement)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasRe.label: has Requested Shipping Priority
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasRe.description: The level of importance or sequence assigned to the delivery. (epo-ord:DeliveryAgreement)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isPar.label: is Partial Delivery Allowed
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isPar.description: An indicator to state if partial delivery is allowed or not. (epo-ord:DeliveryAgreement)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.label: specifies Delivery Agreement Location
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.description: Relation indicating the Location from the Delivery Agreement.

http://purl.org/dc/terms/Location.label: Location
http://purl.org/dc/terms/Location.description: A spatial region or named place.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.label: Monetary Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.description: A number of monetary units specified using a given unit of currency. Additional information: In the pre-award phase of the procurement, all monetary values are considered to exclude VAT based on the Directive 2014/24/EU.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAm.label: has Amount Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAm.description: The numeric value of the amount, including decimals. (epo:MonetaryValue)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.label: has Currency
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.description: The identifier of the currency as in the standard code list used.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.label: has Currency Code List Agency ID
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.description: Identifier of the agency that maintains the currency code list used. (epo:MonetaryValue)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.label: has Currency Code List Agency Name
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.description: Name of the agency that maintains the currency code list used. (epo:MonetaryValue)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.label: has Currency Code List ID
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCu.description: Concept scheme URI used for the currency code list. (epo:MonetaryValue)

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Credi.label: Credit Transfer Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Credi.description: Information about a direct payment of money from one bank account into another.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.label: has Payment Account Identifier
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.description: A unique identifier of the financial payment account, at a payment service provider, to which payment should be made.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.label: has Payment Account Name
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.description: The name of the account that can send and receive payments to and from a payment service provider. (epo-inv:CreditTransferInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.label: has Payment Service Provider Identifier
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasPa.description: An identifier for the payment service provider where a payment account is located.

http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.label: Identifier
http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.description: This class is based on the UN/CEFACT Identifier complex type defined in See Section 5.8 of Core Components Data Type Catalogue Version 3.1 (http://www.unece.org/fileadmin/DAM/cefact/codesfortrade/CCTS/CCTS-DTCatalogueVersion3p1.pdf) In RDF this is expressed using the following properties: - the content string should be provided using skos:notation, datatyped with the identifier scheme (inclduing the version number if appropriate); - use dcterms:creator to link to a class describing the agency that manages the identifier scheme or adms:schemaAgency to provide the name as a literal. Although not part of the ADMS conceptual model, it may be useful to provide further properties to the Identifier class such as dcterms:created to provide the date on which the identifier was issued.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quali.label: Quality Target Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quali.description: Information about a specific performance goal.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.label: has Quality Target Code
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.description: Relation indicating the type of performance goal.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.label: has Quality Target Description
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.description: Explanatory text about the performance goal. (epo:QualityTargetInformation)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Contr.label: Contract Term
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Contr.description: Conditions and stipulations defining particularities of the Post Award Phase.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Evalu.label: Evaluator
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Evalu.description: A Role of an Agent that asses the Tender submitted for a Lot.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#playe.label: played By
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#playe.description: Relation indicating the Agent that fulfills the function of the instance of the concept.

http://www.w3.org/ns/person#Person.label: Person
http://www.w3.org/ns/person#Person.description: An individual person who may be dead or alive, but not imaginary. It is that restriction that makes person:Person a sub class of both foaf:Person and schema:Person which both cover imaginary characters as well as real people.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Recei.label: Receipt Advice Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Recei.description: Details concerning the Fulfilment of a Despatch Line.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSub.label: is Submitted For Despatch Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSub.description: Relation indicating that the instance of the concept is sent for a specific Despatch Line.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Despa.label: Despatch Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Despa.description: Details concerning the fulfilment of an Order Line.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quant.label: Quantity
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quant.description: A counted number of non-monetary units possibly including fractions.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotGr.label: Lot Group Award Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotGr.description: Award information related to a given Group of Lots.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAp.label: has Approximate Group Framework Agreement Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAp.description: Relation indicating the estimated Monetary Value of the Lot Group within the Framework Agreement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasGr.label: has Group Framework Agreement Maximum Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasGr.description: Relation indicating the maximum Monetary Value of the Lot Group within the Framework Agreement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotGr.label: Lot Group
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#LotGr.description: Combination of several Lots to conduct comparative assessment of the Tenders. Additional Information: The comparative assessment may refer to the Selection Criteria, Award and Value that apply to several Lots. Pending of discussion with eForms Member States may provide that, where more than one lot may be awarded to the same tenderer, contracting authorities may award contracts combining several or all lots where they have specified in the contract notice or in the invitation to confirm interest that they reserve the possibility of doing so and indicate the lots or groups of lots that may be combined.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.label: Monetary Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.description: A number of monetary units specified using a given unit of currency. Additional information: In the pre-award phase of the procurement, all monetary values are considered to exclude VAT based on the Directive 2014/24/EU.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.label: Certificate
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.description: Proof of conformance of the instance of the concept to a defined set of criteria. Additional Information: Certificates provide assurance of credibility, trust, and transparency in the procurement process.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#attes.label: attested By Label
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#attes.description: Relation indicating which label the certificate is about.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCe.label: has Certification Number
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCe.description: The unique identifier of the certificate. (epo:Certificate)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#issue.label: issued By Certifier
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#issue.description: Relation indicating the Certifier responsible for providing the ItemCertificate. Additional Information: Certifier is a role played by an organisation.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.label: Certification Label
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.description: Concept that stands for a set of norms, expectations, standards and policies. Additional Information: Such standards may relate to social, ethical and quality etc.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.label: Certifier
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Certi.description: The role of an agent who issues a certificate. Additional Information: The certifier is responsible for verifying and confirming the conformance of the instance of the concept to a defined set of criteria.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Change Confirmation
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: A Document providing information on the acknowledgment of the Order Change.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#respo.label: responds To Order Change
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#respo.description: Takes action in reaction to an Order Change.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.label: specifies Buyer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.description: Relation indicating that the instance of the concept involves the Buyer.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.label: specifies Seller
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.description: Relation indicating that the instance of the concept involves the Seller.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Buyer.label: Buyer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Buyer.description: A Role of an Agent that awards a Contract and/or purchases items. Additional information: In Public Procurement the Role of Buyer is carried out by the following types of Organisation: Contracting Authority, Contracting Entity, a defense Contractor, an international Organisation, or an organisation awarding a Contract subsidized by a Contracting Authority. In Pre-Award, the Buyer generally awards the Contract, however future purchasers may be foreseen. In Post-Award the buyer generally refers to the purchaser of items.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Change
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: A Document providing information on the formal request from the Buyer to the Seller specifying the requested change in goods, services or works to be delivered in respect to a previous Order.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Selle.label: Seller
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Selle.description: A Role of an Agent who transfers the ownership of the Procurement results (goods, services or works) to the Buyer. Additional information: A Role of an Agent that sells a Procurement Result (goods, services or work) to a Buyer. The Seller is bound by a Contract i.e. it has legal responsibility. The Seller may or may not be the same as the Supplier. The Seller may or may not issue the Invoice. The Seller may or may not be the owner of the credit owed by the Buyer.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Profe.label: Professional Suitability Summary
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Profe.description: Overview of the professional ability required for the economic operator to execute the contract.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#descr.label: describes Profession Relevant Law
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#descr.description: Explanatory text about the law concerning the profession. (epo:ProfessionalSuitabilitySummary)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSe.label: has Service Reserved To Particular Profession
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSe.description: Execution of the contract is limited to a particular profession. (epo:ProfessionalSuitabilitySummary)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSpe.label: is Specific To Order Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSpe.description: Information that is particular to one or multiple Order Lines.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Contr.label: Contract
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Contr.description: A voluntary, deliberate, and legally binding agreement between two or more competent parties.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: Details concerning a given unit of goods, services or works requested in the Order. Additional information: In general, an Order Line contains the Quantity and Price of the goods, services and goods requested in the Order. However, in certain cases, the Price may not be known, as the Price may fluctuate from one day to the other.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Agreement
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: A Document sent from the Seller to Buyer specifying the goods, services or works to be delivered concerning a specific Order.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Agreement Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: Details concerning a given unit of goods, services or works mentioned in the Order Agreement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Offer.label: Offer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Offer.description: Document providing the Monetary Value and the details to fulfill the Requirements set out in the Procurement Documents or Request for Offer. Additional information: A quotation is considered to be an Offer in the eProcurement Ontology.

http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Evidence.label: Evidence
http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Evidence.description: The Evidence class is defined in the Core Criterion and Core Evidence vocabulary (CCCEV) as any resource that can prove that a specific requirement is met.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Batch.label: Batch
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Batch.description: A production lot in which the Item instances were produced.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBa.label: has Batch ID
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBa.description: The identifier assigned to a specific batch of the produced Item.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBe.label: has Best Before Date
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBe.description: The day until which the quality of the item will remain optimal. (epo-cat:Batch)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEx.label: has Expiry Date
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEx.description: The last day until which the item can be used. (epo-cat:Batch)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isPro.label: is Production Of
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isPro.description: Relation indicating the Item that is manufactured.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Abstr.label: Abstract Item
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Abstr.description: Gathering class for the Items, Item Requests and Deliverables. Additional information: The subclasses have in common that they are related to a procurement need.

http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.label: Identifier
http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.description: This class is based on the UN/CEFACT Identifier complex type defined in See Section 5.8 of Core Components Data Type Catalogue Version 3.1 (http://www.unece.org/fileadmin/DAM/cefact/codesfortrade/CCTS/CCTS-DTCatalogueVersion3p1.pdf) In RDF this is expressed using the following properties: - the content string should be provided using skos:notation, datatyped with the identifier scheme (inclduing the version number if appropriate); - use dcterms:creator to link to a class describing the agency that manages the identifier scheme or adms:schemaAgency to provide the name as a literal. Although not part of the ADMS conceptual model, it may be useful to provide further properties to the Identifier class such as dcterms:created to provide the date on which the identifier was issued.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEs.label: has Estimated Percentage
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasEs.description: The estimated proportion foreseen to be subcontracted. (epo:SubcontractingEstimate)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSu.label: has Subcontracting Estimated Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSu.description: The estimated value of a single subcontract.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSu.label: has Subject Matter
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasSu.description: Description of the share of the contract that is to be subcontracted. Additional information: This can be an aggregate of several subcontracts. (epo:SubcontractingEstimate)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSub.label: is Subcontracting Percentage Known
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSub.description: Indicates whether the ratio of the Subcontracting Estimate is defined. (epo:SubcontractingEstimate)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSub.label: is Subcontracting Value Known
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isSub.description: Indicates whether the amount of the Subcontracting Estimate is defined. (epo:SubcontractingEstimate)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.label: Monetary Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.description: A number of monetary units specified using a given unit of currency. Additional information: In the pre-award phase of the procurement, all monetary values are considered to exclude VAT based on the Directive 2014/24/EU.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAd.label: has Additional Information
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAd.description: Supplementary data about the instance of the concept. (epo-cat:Line)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCo.label: has Constraint
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasCo.description: Relation indicating that the instance of the concept has a Constraint.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasFo.label: has Formula
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasFo.description: The mathematical equation or any other description used for complicated weighing of criteria (e.g. non-linear weighing, the analytic hierarchy process) when a weighing cannot be expressed per criterion. (epo:ProcurementCriterion)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasWe.label: has Weight Value Type
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasWe.description: No definition. Waiting on CCCEV alignment.

http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Constraint.label: Constraint
http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Constraint.description: Limitation applied to an Information Concept.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Despa.label: Despatch Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Despa.description: Details concerning the fulfilment of an Order Line.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDe.label: has Despatched Quantity
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDe.description: The Quantity that has been sent.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasOu.label: has Outstanding Quantity
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasOu.description: The Quantity of non-despatched Goods Items.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasOu.label: has Outstanding Quantity Reason
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasOu.description: Explanation of the outstanding quantity still to be despatched. (epo-ful:DespatchLine)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.label: Order Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Order.description: Details concerning a given unit of goods, services or works requested in the Order. Additional information: In general, an Order Line contains the Quantity and Price of the goods, services and goods requested in the Order. However, in certain cases, the Price may not be known, as the Price may fluctuate from one day to the other.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quant.label: Quantity
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quant.description: A counted number of non-monetary units possibly including fractions.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Goods.label: Goods Item
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Goods.description: An Item including its initial packaging during transportation. Additional information: For example, the Item being bought is the computer. The weight of the computer is X. The weight of the Goods Item is the weight of the computer plus the weight of the initial packaging. During transportation you have for example trace ID which is applied to the GoodsItem, rather than on the Item itself. The declared statistical value can only apply once you have a GoodsItem.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDe.label: has Declared Statistical Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasDe.description: The Monetary Value declared for statistical purposes.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasTr.label: has Trace ID
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasTr.description: The identifier used for tracking the Goods Item. Additional information: An example is the EPC number used in RFID.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Despa.label: Despatch Line
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Despa.description: Details concerning the fulfilment of an Order Line.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.label: Monetary Value
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Monet.description: A number of monetary units specified using a given unit of currency. Additional information: In the pre-award phase of the procurement, all monetary values are considered to exclude VAT based on the Directive 2014/24/EU.

http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.label: Identifier
http://www.w3.org/ns/adms#Identifier.description: This class is based on the UN/CEFACT Identifier complex type defined in See Section 5.8 of Core Components Data Type Catalogue Version 3.1 (http://www.unece.org/fileadmin/DAM/cefact/codesfortrade/CCTS/CCTS-DTCatalogueVersion3p1.pdf) In RDF this is expressed using the following properties: - the content string should be provided using skos:notation, datatyped with the identifier scheme (inclduing the version number if appropriate); - use dcterms:creator to link to a class describing the agency that manages the identifier scheme or adms:schemaAgency to provide the name as a literal. Although not part of the ADMS conceptual model, it may be useful to provide further properties to the Identifier class such as dcterms:created to provide the date on which the identifier was issued.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Respo.label: Response
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Respo.description: An answer given to a question that is part of an ESPD Request.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#answe.label: answers Question
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#answe.description: Relation indicating that the Response replies to a question.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isRei.label: is Reinforced By
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isRei.description: Relation indicating an Evidence that supports the Response.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#refer.label: refers To Applicable Period
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#refer.description: Relation indicating the period to which the Response shall apply.

http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Evidence.label: Evidence
http://data.europa.eu/m8g/Evidence.description: The Evidence class is defined in the Core Criterion and Core Evidence vocabulary (CCCEV) as any resource that can prove that a specific requirement is met.

http://data.europa.eu/m8g/InformationReq.label: Information Requirement
http://data.europa.eu/m8g/InformationReq.description: Requested data that is to be proven by Evidence.

http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Interval.label: Time interval
http://www.w3.org/2006/time#Interval.description: A temporal entity with an extent or duration

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Offer.label: Offer Issuer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Offer.description: The Role of an Agent that distributes an Offer.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Offer.label: Offer
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Offer.description: Document providing the Monetary Value and the details to fulfill the Requirements set out in the Procurement Documents or Request for Offer. Additional information: A quotation is considered to be an Offer in the eProcurement Ontology.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#ESPD.label: ESPD
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#ESPD.description: A self-declaration of an economic operator, stating that it does not fall under the Exclusion Grounds and that it meets the Selection Criteria for a specific procurement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isCon.label: is Contained In Tender
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isCon.description: Relation indicating that the instance of the concept is part of a Tender.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#provi.label: provides Information On
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#provi.description: Offers information about an instance of a concept.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#relat.label: relates To ESPD Request
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#relat.description: Is about an European Single Procurement Document (ESPD) Request.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.label: specifies Response
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#speci.description: Relation indicating that an ESPD Response contains a Response.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#ESPDR.label: ESPD Request
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#ESPDR.description: A document issued by a Buyer defining how the Exclusion Grounds and the Selection Criteria should be addressed in an European Single Procurement Document for a specific procurement.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Respo.label: Response
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Respo.description: An answer given to a question that is part of an ESPD Request.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Tende.label: Tender
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Tende.description: Information submitted by the Economic Operator to specify its Offer regarding a specific Lot, in response to the call for Tender.

http://www.w3.org/ns/org#Organization.label: Organization
http://www.w3.org/ns/org#Organization.description: Represents a collection of people organized together into a community or other social, commercial or political structure. The group has some common purpose or reason for existence which goes beyond the set of people belonging to it and can act as an Agent. Organizations are often decomposable into hierarchical structures.  It is recommended that SKOS lexical labels should be used to label the Organization. In particular `skos:prefLabel` for the primary (possibly legally recognized name), `skos:altLabel` for alternative names (trading names, colloquial names) and `skos:notation` to denote a code from a code list. Alternative names: _Collective_ _Body_ _Org_ _Group_

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Busin.label: Business
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Busin.description: A private law company registered in a national registry.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBe.label: has Beneficial Owner
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBe.description: A role of any natural person(s) who ultimately owns or controls the organisation or on whose behalf a transaction or activity is being conducted. Additional Information: This role is defined in the directive EU 2015/849 and it's beyond the scope for public eProcurement domain.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBu.label: has Business Size
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasBu.description: The category of the business depending on number of employees and turnover. Additional information: See Commission Recommendation of 6 May 2003 concerning the definition of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isLis.label: is Listed Company
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#isLis.description: Public companies listed on a stock exchange and subject to disclosure requirements (either by stock exchange rules or through law or enforceable means), which impose requirements to ensure adequate transparency of beneficial ownership. <u>Additional Information:</u> In eForms this indicator is used when the nationality of beneficial owner is not provided because the Economic Operator is registered in a regulated market that is subject to disclosure requirements consistent with Union law or subject to equivalent international standards which ensure adequate transparency of ownership information. (epo:Business)

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://www.w3.org/ns/person#Person.label: Person
http://www.w3.org/ns/person#Person.description: An individual person who may be dead or alive, but not imaginary. It is that restriction that makes person:Person a sub class of both foaf:Person and schema:Person which both cover imaginary characters as well as real people.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Award.label: Award Criterion
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Award.description: Criterion that describes a Requirement that the Tender needs to resolve and on which the Tender is evaluated and ranked.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAw.label: has Award Criteria Stated In Procurement Documents
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasAw.description: The Award Criteria are provided in the Procurement Documents. (epo:AwardCriterion)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasFi.label: has Fixed Value Type
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasFi.description: The method to interpret the fixed value as pertaining to a total or unit.

http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.label: Concept
http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Conc.description: An idea or notion; a unit of thought.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.label: has Qualification Criteria Stated In ESPD Request
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.description: The Qualification Criteria is specified in the ESPD Request. (epo:QualificationCriterion)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.label: has Qualification Criteria Stated In Notice
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.description: The Qualification Criteria is specified in the Notice. (epo:QualificationCriterion)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.label: has Qualification Criteria Stated In Procurement Documents
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#hasQu.description: The Qualification Criteria is specified in the Procurement Documents. (epo:QualificationCriterion)

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#inclu.label: includes National Criterion
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#inclu.description: Relation indicating that a Criterion contains a Criterion that is specific to a given Member State.

http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quali.label: Qualification Criterion
http://data.europa.eu/a4g/ontology#Quali.description: Criterion used in the first stage of procurement.
